title: Pseudo-class: where the element stands
checkLabels.t5: All five list items are there
checkLabels.t12: The heading is still dark red
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. Every line of the list still looks the same.
hint2.untouched: You only work in styles.css. The HTML stays as it is.
why.not-closed: A closing curly or round bracket is missing after the pseudo-class.
hint2.not-closed: nth-child and not bring their own round brackets. Open one, close one.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.lost-items: There are no longer five list items. You write a rule, you do not delete content.
why.lost-p: There are no longer two paragraphs.
why.added-class: You added a class label in the HTML. That is exactly what this lesson avoids.
hint2.added-class: First and last change on their own the moment you add a line. A label does not.
hint3.added-class: Tomorrow Bean Street adds a new tea. If the first line is marked by hand, you have to move the label. If it is picked by position, you do not.
hint4.added-class: Remove the class attributes. Ask where the element stands among its siblings.
why.no-first: There is no list item at all.
why.first-not-bold: The first item is not bold.
hint2.first-not-bold: The condition attaches to the selector with a colon, no space.
why.no-last: There is no last list item.
why.last-not-italic: The note at the bottom is not italic.
why.no-intro: There is no paragraph at all. The HTML has been rearranged.
why.intro-not-teal: The first paragraph is not teal. If you wrote a rule and nothing happened, it matches nothing.
hint2.intro-not-teal: Check which element is the FIRST child of body. Not the first paragraph — the first child.
hint3.intro-not-teal: The first child of body is the h1. Then an h2. The paragraph is only fourth. So p:first-child is an empty set. The browser never complains about a rule that matches nothing — it just walks past it.
hint4.intro-not-teal: You are not asking for the first child, but for the first of its kind. The condition is called first-of-type.
why.second-p-changed: The second paragraph changed too. It stays saddlebrown.
hint2.second-p-changed: The p rule covers both. Change it and you change both.
why.h1-broken: The heading is no longer dark red. You deleted the h1 rule.
blocks.0.text: The Bean Street menu is a list. The last line is not a price — it is a note saying tax is included.
blocks.2.text: You want the first line bold and the note at the bottom italic. You write a rule for li and get this:
blocks.4.text: All five lines turn bold. A class would work again, but think about what follows: tomorrow you add a new tea at the top and you have to move the label by hand. First and last are not properties of the line. They are its position — and position changes on its own.
blocks.6.text: A colon attaches a condition to the selector you already wrote.
blocks.8.text: A pseudo-class is not a new selector. It is a condition about the same element — not what it is, but where it stands. That is why it is written with no space: a space already means something else.
blocks.9.text: A class says what the element is. A pseudo-class says where it stands.
blocks.10.text: The trap in the word child
blocks.11.text: first-child means the first child of its parent, whatever tag that is. It does not mean the first paragraph. If a heading comes before the paragraph, the heading is the first child and the paragraph rule matches nothing. No error, no warning — nothing happens:
blocks.13.text: For the first of a given kind there is a separate condition: first-of-type. Both count elements only — blank lines and text between tags do not count. There are also pseudo-classes for state, like hover and focus. They get their own section later.
blocks.15.text: Three things, all in styles.css. The first menu line becomes bold with font-weight: bold. The last line becomes italic with font-style: italic. The first paragraph at the top becomes teal, the second stays saddlebrown. Do not touch the HTML.
